chapter_title.ar: باب بَدْءِ الْأَذَانِ وَ الْإِقَامَةِ وَ فَضْلِهِمَا وَ ثَوَابِهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 18 – The Azān (Call to the Salāt), and the Iqamah (Call to the establishment of the Salāt), and their merits and their Rewards

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/91)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/91)
text: أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) كَانَ يُؤَذِّنُ وَ يُقِيمُ غَيْرُهُ وَ قَالَ كَانَ يُقِيمُ وَ قَدْ أَذَّنَ غَيْرُهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ali Bin Mahziyar, from one of our companions, from Ismail Bin Jabir that,
‘Abu Abdullahasws would recite an Azān, and someone else would recite the Iqamah, and he said, ‘When heasws recited the Iqamah, someone else would recite the Azān’.
en.sarwar: 25. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Ali ibn Mahziyar from certain persons of our people from ’Isma‘il ibn Jabir who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would say Adhan and someone else would say ‘Iqamah.’ He (the narrator) has said that Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would say ‘Iqamah and someone else would say Adhan.’”
